info.name: Turtle Tower
info.attribution:
Layout, design, coding, and library image by LawDawg96. Released to the Public Domain under CC0.

Turtle icon by Pong Pong available under CC BY 3.0 from https://thenounproject.com/icon/turtle-7942139/.

Rocks icon by Adriana Danaila available CC By 3.0 from https://thenounproject.com/icon/rocks-856718/.

All other icons have attribution as listed in the About overlay.

Background image by GuentherDillingen available under the Pixabay License from https://pixabay.com/photos/water-lilies-water-lilies-water-plants-4386950/.
info.description: A troop of turtles trek across the track, frequently climbing on top of each other to take a turtle taxi because trudging is terribly overrated. Throngs of thrilled yet totally clueless spectators toss tokens trying to tell which turtle will triumph.
info.similarAwards:
2022 Geek Media Awards Game of the Year for Beginners Winner
2020 Gra Roku Best Party Game Nominee
2016 Hungarian Board Game Award Nominee
2015 Nederlandse Spellenprijs Best Family Game Nominee

info.helpText:
Players sit in seats. Each seat is represented by an animal icon used on cards and tokens in the game.

Press the Reset button (restart_alt) to begin. Choose whether to use the counterclockwise black and white turtles. Then the board will be set up and a random player selected to start. If playing with 6 or more, the partnership tokens will also be made available.

Other usability information is found at the bottom right of the screen by hovering over the information boxes.

fields.text: This will completely reset the board and start a new game. Are you sure?

clickRoutine: var playerCount = length ${activeSeats}

arguments.addendum: ${playerName} took a ${vp} ticket

clickRoutine: // If you always want this seat to be a specific color, add a `staticColor` property wtih the hex color as the value

// If you want this seat to do other things, scroll all the way to the bottom and lock for comments about adding functions depending on whether the seat is being used or not. If you need functions that activate whenever there is a status change (being used or not), add those at the end of this clickRoutine.
// Check if another player is picking this seat

thenRoutine: // If another player is currently picking this seat, notify the play and end

fields.text: Pick another seat. Another player is getting this one ready.

elseRoutine: // If another player does not have the INPUT overlay on this seat active then flag it as active and CALL the routines needed to use the seat
// Update player color and name to match player's selections.

var result = ${PROPERTY colorArray}
// Remove colors used in other seats from the list of available colors for this player's overlay choices

elseRoutine.header: Enter your name and choice of player color.

thenRoutine: // If seat is occupied by the player pressing the button, allow player to change name, color if not static, or get up from seat.

elseRoutine.header: Enter/change your name and choice of player color (or leave seat).

elseRoutine: // If seat is occupied by other than the player clicking, then confirm removal.

elseRoutine.header: Are you sure you want to remove ${PROPERTY player} from this seat?

elseRoutine: // Evaluate scenarios for what properties need to change

thenRoutine: // Double check to make sure that another player has not chosen the same color if multiple seat INPUT overlays were open at the same time

elseRoutine: // If there is anything else this seat needs to do after it has been claimed, do that here.

fields.label: Type the number of points gained or lost. Put a - before the number to lose points.

tipsTurnActions.detail:
Turn Actions

Choose to take one of the following actions during your turn. At the end of your turn, press the turn buttonto advance the turn indicator to the next player.

Betting Ticket: Take 1 betting ticket. These are the thin cards in the middle of the track with a colored turtleat the top. Place it in the holder next to your seat.

Spectator Tile: Place your spectator tile (located to the right of the animal icon in your seat area) on an empty place on the track that is not the first space and not adjacent to another spectator tile. Click the tile to flip it over, if desired. If your tile is already on the track, you can move it (and optionally flip it) to a different space with this action.

Roll the Dice: Click the dice icon in the middle of the board. One die will automatically be selected and rolled. Manually move the turtles according to the move rules. The number next to the die in your seat area is increased by 1 for end-of-round scoring. If a turtle lands on a player's spectator tile, that player should immediately add 1 vp to their score. Click a turtle to flip its orientation.

Place Overall Bet: Take 1 card from your hand and place it in the holder marked "1st Place" to predict the winner or "Last Place" to predict the loser. These cards will be evaluated at the end of the game.

Enter Partnership (only with 6+ players): Remove the partnership tile from your seat area (located to the right of the spectator icon) and temporarily place it on the board next to the player you have chosen to be your partner. Take their partnership tile and put it into your seat area and place your tile into their seat area. The cards will automatically flip over to indicate the partnership.

tipsScoring.detail:
End of Round Scoring

A round ends after 5 dice are rolled. If playing with the counterclockwise turtles, one die will not be rolled each round. After rolling the 5th die, move the turtles according to the rules (including scoring vp for landing on a spectator tile). Then score for dice rolls and betting tickets as stated below. To adjust score, click the "vp" button in your seat area and enter the number by which you want your score to change (use a "-" sign to decrease). After scoring is completed, press the next round button to set up the board for the next round. The player after the one that rolled the 5th dice takes the first turn in the next round.

Dice Rolls: Add 1 vp for each time you rolled the dice that round. This is indicated by the number next to the die in your seat area.

Betting tickets: For each betting ticket matching the color of the lead turtle, get the number of vp indicated at the top of the ticket (either 5, 3, or 2). For each betting ticket matching the color of the 2nd place turtle, gain 1 vp. For every other betting ticket, lose 1 vp. Remember, a turtle is in first place if it has advanced the farthest along the track and is on top of any stack of turtles. The black and white turtles are ignored in calculating positions.

End of Game Scoring

The game ends as soon as any one of the colored turtles crosses the line on the track between space 1 and space 16. When that happens, do the normal end-of-round scoring plus overall bet scoring.

Overall Bets: To score for the overall bets on the winning and losing turtles, first press the flip button in the bottom center of the room. This will arrange both holders with face up cards and the top card is the player that placed the first bet. Manually drag cards out of the holder. Players that make incorrect bets for either the overall winner or loser lose 1 vp for each incorrect bet. For correct bets, score is based on the order of the cards in the holder (ignoring any incorrect bets). The first player to bet correctly gets 8 vp, the second correct player gets 5 vp, and so on according to the scoring table between the holders.
